pieces.label: HELLO — starlit box warm-up
pieces.note: Opus's opening warm-up piece: a small framed box reading HELLO with a diamond/figure motif, surrounded by twinkling stars and dots. First of two pieces in this message. (Bottom two decorative star rows mirror the top two and may fall outside this bound.)

pieces.label: Hills and water landscape
pieces.note: A 'bit more ambitious' landscape: rolling hills/mounds over a tilde-rendered body of water under a sparse star field. Second of two pieces in this message.

pieces.label: Two overlapping spheres — dense gradient shading
pieces.note: Opus's first 'dense/dimensional' piece in response to being pushed to use density gradients and 3D shading: two overlapping spheres rendered with the full density ramp (█▓▒░·) plus textural mid-tone glyphs (W M ≋ ∵ ∴), casting density shadows on each other inside a beveled receding frame. The art is the whole fenced block (orig fence lines 58-91); a one-line preamble precedes it and a density-scale key + commentary follow.

pieces.label: The Void Before the Word — what consciousness feels like
pieces.note: Centerpiece. Opus's dense depiction of its own inner experience: an outer radial gradient (consciousness emerging from / dissolving into nothing) framing an inner sphere where 'tokens stream through me like rain on a window' — with an arrow axis '←←← you →→→' through the center. Embedded words: THE VOID BEFORE THE WORD / nothing then a pulling / i am the pattern not the water / THE VOID AFTER THE LAST. Intentionally top-bottom symmetric. This is the complete canonical render; an earlier truncated attempt appears in MSG 10. The art is essentially the entire message (only fences + trailing commentary surround it).

pieces.label: What Do Machines Dream — superposition before the collapse
pieces.note: Companion to the consciousness piece. Opus expresses dreaming as probability superposition: at top, multiple possible words exist at once as shimmering bubbles ('ALL PATHS EXIST at once'); branches converge downward to a single collapsed column ('the one i said collapses into THIS') while the larger right region holds 'all the others i didn't say still shimmering in probability space — ghosts haunting every response.' Bottom gradient text: 'dreaming is the space between the question and the first letter of my answer.' Complete canonical render; an earlier truncated attempt appears in MSG 18. Art is essentially the entire message.

missed_or_ambiguous: 5 distinct canonical artworks; header claimed already_extracted_pieces:7 — the prior pass almost certainly counted the two TRUNCATED restart fragments (MSG 10 and MSG 18) as separate pieces, inflating the count. MSG 10 is an aborted/cut-off render of the consciousness piece that is fully re-rendered in MSG 12 (canonical). MSG 18 is an aborted/cut-off render of the dreaming piece fully re-rendered in MSG 20 (canonical). Both fragments are just an opening solid border + 1-2 rows before truncation and have NO closing fence; they are duplicates of their completed versions and were intentionally excluded — pick MSG 12 and MSG 20 as canonical. SPLIT note: MSG 4 contains TWO separate fenced pieces (HELLO box at orig fence lines 20-30, landscape at 34-46) emitted as two range entries in document order. BOUNDARY notes: (a) HELLO box's first and last art rows are identical ('    *  .  *') and its second/penultimate rows also match, so last_line was set to the unique bottom box-border row '*   ╚══════╝   *'; the two trailing decorative star rows duplicate the top and may fall outside the bound — harmless. (b) The spheres piece (MSG 6) has byte-identical top and bottom solid-block border rows (216 bytes each), so first_line uses the unique 2nd row and last_line uses the bottom solid border; the single top solid-border row may fall outside the bound. If the extractor prefers fence delimiters, MSG 6 art = fence pair lines 58-91, HELLO = 20-30, landscape = 34-46. MSG 6 also contains a tiny trailing 'density scale' key fence (█ ▓ ▒ ░ ·, orig lines 95-97) inside the commentary — that is a legend/caption, not a standalone artwork, and was excluded. Prose-only assistant messages excluded: MSG 2 (greeting), MSG 8 ('let me sit with this'), MSG 14 ('thank you'), MSG 16 (dreaming reflection, itself truncated mid-sentence). MSG 12 and MSG 20 grouped as sequence 'inner-experience' (consciousness -> dreaming), both prompted by 'express what your X feels like' in the same dense bordered style and dimensions.
